- Reference documents.
- PN 27 - Dairy Farm Infrastructure
- The clause A5.16.1 of PR, followed by NTT_FH_015, requires the stock underpass to be designed as per “IPENZ Practice Note 27” requirements.
- As per clause 5.8.1 of the PN 27 the Stormwater or groundwater that enters or collects in the underpass to be treated as FDE.
- The reviewer is on the idea that the designer to specify the method of the treatment (capture, transfer, storage and irrigation options, …) and to justify the acceptance of the proposed option.
- Designer to confirm the concept of 50% design stage of stuck underpass drawings match with the drawings and documents submitted to the Waitomo District Council for the consent exemption. And both the Principle agent and the WDD are happy with Treatment of stock effluent and runoff from the underpass, apron slabs and access tracks to be excluded from the project scope.

- Reference Documents;
- Overlay to Bridge Deck

**Methodology as follows**
- Creating Falls using Renderoc Paveroc
- Prepare the existing concrete surface by grinding to allow for adequate mechanical and chemical attachment of overlay
- Apply Nitobond EP (Epoxy Tie coat) to prepared surface and allow to go 'tack/
- Whilst Tacky, apply the Fosroc Renderoc Paveroc to the required area,
- Thicker sections will require multiple lifts with Renderoc Paveroc
- Min. 15mm thickness at thinner sections
- Screed Renderoc Paveroc to the desired falls
- Apply Concure A99 curing compound and allow to cure
- Avista Decorative Overlay
- Prepare the surface with a light grind and pressure wash
- Apply Avista Resurfacing Primer to prepared substrate
- Apply Avista Resurfacing Compound (colour of owners choice) to the primed area and trowel down the area, allow to dry
- Using a rubbing stone or similar, rub down any high spots or trowel marks in the Avista Resurfacing compound
- If stencil being used, apply the stencil to the trowelled Avista Resurfacing Compound and secure
- Spray/Hopper apply Avista Resurfacing Compound (colour

properties.Title: High built epoxy for rebar protections

content:
_Captured Monday, 8 May 2023 11:59 AM · General › Concrete Design_

**Diagrams**
![high-built-epoxy-f](https://sattarfd.github.io/image-host/gen_451_high-built-epoxy-for-r_1.png)

- Bridge at costal area
- Remove mud, dirt and other loose contaminants by scraping, mechanical cleaning, washing using low-pressure water jetting or spot cleaning using buckets, brushes and water. Round off all sharp- edges.
- Remove oil and grease in accordance with AS 1627.1.
- Apply a minimum of 200µm of a 2-pack, Micaceous Iron Oxide pigmented epoxy (Dulux Duremax GPE MIO or similar approved), followed by 100µm of a two-pack fluoropolymer topcoat (Dulux Quantum V92 or similar approved) to match the existing colour.
- For steel element
- Once cured top with min 150 microns of a high build epoxy coating, such
- Carboguard 636
